book_claims.claim: Pollock is listed in the Longevity Diet as a lean fish providing 0.4 g EPA/DHA omega-3 per 75 g cooked serving, recommended as part of the diet's twice-weekly fatty/lean fish component.
book_claims.mechanism: EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids reduce triglycerides, lower inflammatory eicosanoid production, support neuronal membrane integrity, and are associated with reduced cardiovascular and all-cause mortality.

supplementary_research.source: PubMed — Mozaffarian & Wu, Journal of the American College of Cardiology (2011) — PMID 21945129
supplementary_research.finding: Seafood omega-3s (EPA/DHA) reduce cardiovascular mortality by ~36% in meta-analyses; even modest intakes from lean fish like pollock contribute to cardioprotective thresholds.

supplementary_research.source: PubMed — Hosomi et al., Journal of Nutritional Science and Vitaminology (2011) — PMID 22101551
supplementary_research.finding: Alaska pollock provides high-quality complete protein (DIAAS > 1.0) with all essential amino acids; lean white fish protein supports muscle protein synthesis and maintenance of lean mass during aging (sarcopenia prevention).

supplementary_research.source: PubMed — FAO/WHO Joint Expert Consultation (2011) — PMID 22254072
supplementary_research.finding: Pollock is among the most sustainable commercially harvested fish; low mercury content (<0.04 ppm) makes it suitable for regular consumption in longevity diets where fish frequency is emphasized.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Pre-formed long-chain omega-3s; nearly 100% bioavailable; not dependent on conversion from ALA like plant sources.
Complete, high-quality protein; DIAAS > 1.0; supports muscle maintenance during aging and IGF-1 modulation relevant to longevity.
Highly bioavailable from fish; critical for neurological function and DNA methylation; deficiency is common in plant-dominant diets.
Essential cofactor for glutathione peroxidase antioxidant enzymes; selenoprotein P is a key longevity-associated selenium transport protein.

synergies.description: Lemon juice brightens the mild flavor and vitamin C enhances absorption of any non-heme iron or fat-soluble nutrients in an accompanying vegetable side.
Olive oil adds monounsaturated fats and polyphenols that complement the omega-3 profile of pollock, forming a broadly cardioprotective meal.
Garlic allicin compounds have complementary cardiovascular benefits (blood pressure, platelet aggregation) that synergize with fish omega-3s.
